title: The Minor Poems of the Vernon MS. (edited selection: 95 poems in 93 editorial units)
summary: The pinned General Corpus XML is a normalized copy of the recovered U-M encoding, delivered with its complete raw package and 816-TIFF edition facsimile. The TIFFs reproduce Horstmann's 1892 Part I and Furnivall's 1901 Part II; those editions assemble 95 poems in 93 editorial units through direct manuscript transcription, collation, supplied passages, and print-mediated comparison. The graph keeps all 95 entities, 106 relations, 91 reviewed paths, access routes, local-copy ownership, and rights layers distinct.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Two separately dated EETS parts assemble material from Vernon and numerous additional witnesses and printed intermediaries.
editorial_practices.representation: The aggregate edition presents 95 intellectual poems in 93 numbered/count-once editorial units, plus wrappers and apparatus.
editorial_practices.description: The cataloged unit is an edition-defined selection, not one medieval work, one authorial collection, or the whole Vernon manuscript.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The printed parts contain parallel witness streams, prose arguments, notes, Various Readings, and an Appendix across pages 1-786.
editorial_practices.representation: The electronic encoding serializes those streams and retains repeated page references; its 101 modeled parts are representation units rather than 101 independent poems.
editorial_practices.description: The encoding preserves the edition-defined boundary while requiring separate editorial-unit, intellectual-poem, and XML-part counts.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Part I combines Vernon transcriptions with complete parallel streams, supplied readings, and variant apparatus from manuscripts and earlier print editions.
editorial_practices.representation: The lineage preserves each direct, collated, and print-mediated route at its exact passage scope, including Item XXXIV's first Vernon stream, V2 apparatus, and Furnivall-mediated Cotton stream.
editorial_practices.description: A Vernon-centered editorial headline does not make every Part I reading a direct Vernon transcription.
editorial_practices.scope: Horstmann's 1892 Part I and its separately scoped source streams

editorial_practices.source_feature: The edition states that A. F. Parker collated with the manuscript 'henceforward' from Item XLV.
editorial_practices.representation: The responsibility is conservatively scoped to exactly Items 045-054 and LV nos. 01-28, ending with the Vernon-derived main text.
editorial_practices.description: The inference does not extend Parker's role to LV nos. 29-30, Various Readings, the Appendix, or non-Vernon parallel streams.
editorial_practices.scope: Thirty-eight leaf parts from Item XLV through LV no. 28
